---
title: Gene expression trajectories
date: 2024-07-11
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

To identify genes with conserved and divergent expression trajectories (similar to Sepp et al. Figures 3f, 4a).


## Files and directories

### Code

The code can be found in `<integrated software directory>/find_gene_trajectories.R` and in this document. All code was run using the `scrnaseq_env` conda environment.

### Output

The output directory can be found at `<integrated results directory>/20240711`:

- The `ubc` subdirectory contains the results for the RL-to-UBC lineage cells
- The `gc` subdirectory contains the results for the RL-to-GC lineage cells

Each subdirectory contains the following files:

- `gene_stats.csv`: statistics for each gene
  - mean/variance of each gene, variance to mean ratio (`vmr`), expected variance to mean ratio (`pvmr`)
  - center of mass of each gene (which pseudotime bin each gene is most expressed in)
  - if the gene is overdispersed and is included in the downstream analysis (`select_gene`)
- `scaled_pseudobulk_mat_list.rds`: list of matrices containing scaled pseudobulked gene expression (one matrix per species)
- `scaled_pseudobulk_eset.rds`: ExpressionSet object of the scaled pseudobulked counts
- `mfuzz_raw_output.rds`: output of Mfuzz clustering (including the membership scores of each gene)
- `mfuzz_membership_scores.csv`: cluster membership score of each gene
- `mfuzz_clustering_pval.csv`: gene trajectory p-values for each human/mouse ortholog
  - cluster of the max membership score for human/mouse (`mf_hclust_h/m`, `cluster_human/mouse`)
  - classification of the ortholog (`mf_class`) as "diverged" (`fdr` < 0.05 and `cluster_human != cluster_mouse`), "conserved" (`fdr` > 0.5 and `cluster_human == cluster_mouse`), or "not assigned"

::: {.callout-note}
## Note regarding p-values

While there is a "p-value" column in the `mfuzz_clustering_pval.csv` files because Sepp et al. call it a "p-value", the actual calculation of the "p-value" is more like a similarity score. Specifically, it's the dot product of the cluster membership scores between two orthologs, and a lower score ("p-value") means the trajectories of the orthologs are more dissimilar.

Since the p-value isn't really a p-value, the FDR should not be used.
:::


## Results

Load packages:

```{r}
#| cache: false
#| cache-lazy: false

library(tidyverse)
```

### UBC lineage

```{r}
ubc_clust <- read_csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/icheong/CBL_scRNAseq/results/integrated/20240711/ubc/mfuzz_clustering_pval.csv")
```

Parameters for `mfuzz` clustering:

- `centers` (number of clusters) was set to 4
- `m` (fuzziness of the clusters) was set to 1.7284 (see log file)
  - this was selected by running `mestimate`

We can plot the clustered trajectories:

::: {#fig-ubc-gene-clusters}
![](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/icheong/CBL_scRNAseq/results/integrated/20240711/ubc/mfuzz_plot.png)

Plots showing the gene expression trajectories in each of the clusters. The genes with a higher membership score for that specific cluster are shown in pink/magenta.
:::

Let's see the top diverged genes in the UBC lineage:

```{r}
ubc_diverged <- ubc_clust %>%
  filter(mf_class == "diverged") %>%
  arrange(pval)
head(ubc_diverged, n = 10)
```

Using the default parameters for classifying the genes as diverged/conserved, none of the lineage genes were classified as diverged. However, the genes were classified using the FDR which should not be used as the p-value is more like a similarity score (see "Note regarding p-values" above).

Interestingly, the distribution of the similarity scores does look like a p-value histogram (@fig-ubc-orth-similarity). The distribution of the scores for the UBC lineage appears to be bimodal.

```{r}
#| label: fig-ubc-orth-similarity
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of the similarity scores (\"p-values\") between ortholog trajectories in the UBC lineage."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6
#| code-fold: true

.plt <- ggplot(
  data = ubc_clust,
  mapping = aes(pval)
) + 
  geom_histogram() + 
  labs(x = "ortholog similarity", y = "number of orthologs", caption = "UBC lineage") + 
  theme_classic()

.plt
```

### GC lineage

```{r}
gc_clust <- read_csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/icheong/CBL_scRNAseq/results/integrated/20240711/gc/mfuzz_clustering_pval.csv")
```

Parameters for `mfuzz` clustering:

- `centers` (number of clusters) was set to 4
- `m` (fuzziness of the clusters) was set to 1.3606 (see log file)
  - this was selected by running `mestimate`

We can plot the clustered trajectories:

::: {#fig-gc-gene-clusters}
![](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/icheong/CBL_scRNAseq/results/integrated/20240711/gc/mfuzz_plot.png)

Plots showing the gene expression trajectories in each of the clusters. The genes with a higher membership score for that specific cluster are shown in pink/magenta.
:::

Let's see the top diverged genes in the GC lineage:

```{r}
gc_diverged <- gc_clust %>%
  filter(mf_class == "diverged") %>%
  arrange(pval)
head(gc_diverged, n = 10)
```

Like with the UBC lineage, the distribution of the similarity scores for the GC lineage also looks like a p-value histogram (@fig-gc-orth-similarity).

```{r}
#| label: fig-gc-orth-similarity
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of the similarity scores (\"p-values\") between ortholog trajectories in the GC lineage."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6
#| code-fold: true

.plt <- ggplot(
  data = gc_clust,
  mapping = aes(pval)
) + 
  geom_histogram() + 
  labs(x = "ortholog similarity", y = "number of orthologs", caption = "GC lineage") + 
  theme_classic()

.plt
```


## Next steps

Some things to fix/run next time:

- get rid of the FDR correction as the "p-value" isn't really a p-value (see "Note regarding p-values" above)
- set a similarity score aka "p-value" cutoff (e.g. < 0.2) to filter out genes and see how those diverged
- check scaled expression of:
  - some of the diverged genes
  - some of the genes with high similarity
